description_long: This is a bulbous, double-handled ceramic vase with a tall, narrow neck that flares outward at the rim. The body is decorated with intricate geometric patterns, including vertical bands of diamonds and checkerboard motifs, rendered in earthy tones of brown, beige, and black. The handles are small, rounded, and attached symmetrically on opposite sides of the widest part of the body. The neck features horizontal striping, while the lower portion of the vase is more sparsely decorated, revealing the natural clay color. The surface appears matte with some subtle gloss, suggesting a fired ceramic finish. This vase exudes an artisanal, possibly traditional or ethnographic aesthetic, suitable for display in urban public or private spaces.
description: A bulbous, double-handled ceramic vase with geometric patterns in earthy tones and a tall, flared neck. The surface is matte with subtle gloss, and the design is both decorative and traditional.
description_view_0: Front view showing the most detailed geometric patterns and both handles clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rotated view highlighting the transition between patterned and plain surfaces, with one handle partially obscured.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the plain, unadorned clay surface and both handles visible.
description_view_3: Side view showing the boundary between decorated and undecorated areas, with one handle in profile.
